skill.name: evaluation
skill.description: Design evaluation rubrics, test sets, scoring methods, and monitoring patterns for agent systems and LLM workflows.
skill.summary: Build practical evaluation frameworks for agent quality, regressions, and production monitoring.
skill.author: muratcankoylan

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and sensitive-file indicators, but review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences, evaluation score labels, and ordinary Python examples. The skill includes a Python evaluation helper and monitoring examples that can retain query and output previews in memory, so publication is acceptable with a low privacy and script-awareness warning.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Alerts
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby-style shell execution, but the cited locations are Markdown code fences and Python examples. I found no evidence of executable shell backticks or command invocation in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain fenced Markdown examples and Python snippets, not shell execution syntax. No user-controlled command construction or subprocess call appears in those cited blocks.

low_findings.title: False Positive Cryptography and Sensitive File Alerts
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography and certificate or key material. The cited lines describe evaluation concepts, rubric labels, model references, or ordinary Python data structures, and I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, private keys, certificates, or secret material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is text about evaluation methods and sample rubric data. The reviewed context does not contain cryptographic API usage or embedded sensitive files.

low_findings.title: Production Monitoring Examples Retain Interaction Previews
low_findings.description: The production monitoring examples keep truncated query and output previews in memory. This is not exfiltration, but implementers should avoid storing sensitive user data without retention and privacy controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly records query and output previews, but only in local memory and without network transmission. The risk depends on how users adapt the example in production.

content.value_statement: Agent teams need repeatable ways to measure quality, regressions, and production drift. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design rubrics, test sets, and monitoring workflows.
actual_capabilities: Defines multi-dimensional rubrics for factual accuracy, completeness, citations, source quality, and tool efficiency.
Guides test set design across simple, medium, complex, and extended agent tasks.
Explains LLM-as-judge, human review, and end-state evaluation methods.
Provides sample Python structures for scoring, test set management, and evaluation runners.
Shows production monitoring patterns with pass-rate thresholds and quality alerts.
limitations: The included Python evaluator uses simple heuristics and is not a complete production judge.
It does not provide benchmark datasets or validated ground truth labels.
Teams must adapt scoring weights and pass thresholds to their own risk tolerance.
Production monitoring examples need privacy controls before handling sensitive user data.

use_cases.title: Create Regression Tests for an Agent
use_cases.description: Build a test set and scoring rubric before changing prompts, tools, or context strategy.

use_cases.title: Review Output Quality at Scale
use_cases.description: Use LLM-as-judge and human sampling to measure factuality, completeness, and citation quality.

use_cases.title: Monitor Production Agent Health
use_cases.description: Track pass rates, average quality scores, and alert thresholds for sampled production interactions.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Rubric
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new agent evaluation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple evaluation rubric for my agent. Include dimensions, scoring levels, weights, and a pass threshold.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Test Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Create representative cases from real tasks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a test set for this agent workflow. Include simple, medium, complex, and edge-case tasks with expected outcomes.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Agent Versions
prompt_templates.scenario: Measure changes after prompt, model, or tool updates.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me compare two agent configurations. Define metrics, evaluation procedure, failure analysis, and release criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Design Production Monitoring
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced quality gates for deployed systems.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a production evaluation pipeline for sampled agent interactions. Include privacy controls, alert thresholds, dashboards, and human review loops.

output_examples.input: Evaluate a research agent that answers regulatory questions.
output_examples.output: A weighted rubric covering factual accuracy, source authority, citation match, completeness, and escalation behavior.

output_examples.input: Build quality gates before deploying a support agent update.
output_examples.output: A release checklist with baseline scores, regression thresholds, sampled human review, and rollback criteria.

output_examples.input: Monitor production agent performance over time.
output_examples.output: A monitoring plan with sampled evaluations, pass-rate alerts, trend summaries, and privacy-safe preview handling.

best_practices: Evaluate outcomes and final state, not only the exact path an agent followed.
Use multiple quality dimensions with explicit weights and clear pass thresholds.
Combine automated judging with human review for edge cases and high-risk workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on one overall score without dimension-level failure analysis.
Do not test only easy examples that are unlike real production tasks.
Do not store raw production conversations without privacy and retention controls.

faq.question: What does this skill evaluate?
faq.answer: It helps evaluate agent outputs, tool use, citations, completeness, and production quality trends.

faq.question: Can it replace human review?
faq.answer: No. It recommends human review for edge cases, high-risk outputs, and calibration of automated judges.

faq.question: Does it include runnable code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Python helper script with sample evaluator, test set, runner, and monitoring classes.

faq.question: Is the evaluator production ready?
faq.answer: No. The script uses simple heuristics and should be adapted before production use.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What security caution applies?
faq.answer: Review the Python helper before running it and add privacy controls before storing production query or output previews.
